Title: Hot Topics in AI Governance: Legal Perspectives on Risk, Trust, and Responsibility
Description: This dynamic panel will highlight the committees mission to explore the intersection of risk management and trust in the rapidly evolving field of Artificial Intelligence.

Title: Congressional Series: AI and Deepfakes
Webinar Series Title: Task Force on Law and Artificial Intelligence Webinars
Description: Explores the emerging legal, ethical, and regulatory challenges posed by deepfakes. Industry experts and legislators discuss the adoption of legal tools to identify deepfake content, safeguard democratic processes, protect individual rights, and propose frameworks for accountability in both political and judicial arenas.

Title: How Generative AI Can Improve Access to Justice
Webinar Series Title: Task Force on Law and Artificial Intelligence Webinars
Description: Highlights real-world use cases where generative AI boosts legal-aid efficiency and empowers self-represented litigants. The session showcases AI tools that produce understandable, accurate legal documents, demonstrating how such technology helps legal aid attorneys serve more clients and delivers legal guidance directly to underserved communities.

Title: Behind Closed Doors: Judges Unveil the Role of AI in Chambers
Webinar Series Title: Task Force on Law and Artificial Intelligence Webinars
Description: Offers a rare inside view into how judges are incorporating generative AI tools in their chambers. Through candid judicial insights, the webinar reveals how AI assists with case analysis, memo drafting, and research—while addressing issues like confidentiality, ethical considerations, and maintaining judicial discretion.

Title: Do We Need New Rules of Evidence for AI-Generated Evidence?
Webinar Series Title: Task Force on Law and Artificial Intelligence Webinars
Description: Examines whether AI-generated outputs, from acknowledged uses to covert deepfakes, require updates to the Federal Rules of Evidence. The panel evaluates competing proposals, considering how to assess authenticity, reliability, and admissibility of AI-based evidence in court proceedings.

Title: Emerging Policy and Legal Issues at the Intersection of AI & Privacy
Description: the Media & Technology Committee and a panel of experts explore policy and legal issues at the intersection of AI and privacy.

Title: AI Governance: A Conversation with Michael Chertoff and Miriam Vogel
Webinar Series Title: Task Force on Law and Artificial Intelligence Webinars
Description: Features a “fireside chat” with former Homeland Security Secretary Michael Chertoff and Miriam Vogel discussing the evolving intersection of AI governance and legal accountability. They weigh policy frameworks, global regulatory trends, trust-building, and the ethical obligations of AI in public safety and civil rights.

Sponsor/Group: Real Property, Trust, and Estate Law Section
Title: Professors Corner - AI is My Paralegal: Uses of New Technology in Practice
Description: Explore the features, benefits, and challenges of the latest AI products. Discover how these innovations can enhance the practice of trusts & estates and real estate law.

Title: Antitrust Enforcement in Artificial Intelligence: How Would Economists Regulate AI?
Description: The committee discusses recent AI enforcement and the economic concepts of dealing with new AI challenges.

Title: Mastering Generative AI: A Lawyers Guide
Description: Explore how generative AI is transforming transactional law. Learn strategies to evaluate and adopt AI tools, upskill your team, and stay ahead in a competitive market.

Title: AI Ethics in Practice: A Compass for Legal Innovation
Description: This session delves into the ethical dimensions of Generative AI, providing legal professionals with essential guidance to navigate ethical challenges and apply existing rules to AI-generated evidence, emerging legal claims, and more.

Title: Cloud/Privacy: Legal Implications in AI
Description: This panel discusses the concepts of cloud technology including Artificial Intelligence usage in the US and Abroad.

Sponsor/Group: Section of Civil Rights and Social Justice
Title: Going Global: Seeking Redress against Digital Harms beyond the US
Webinar Series Title: AI and the Legal Profession: Navigating Opportunities and Challenges Webinar Series
Description: This webinar provides an overview of the pitfalls of AI and explores pathways to seeking redress for AI-related human rights harms in international/foreign jurisdictions.

Sponsor/Group: Section of Civil Rights and Social Justice
Title: AI in Housing and Benefits: Automating Discrimination, Enhancing Surveillance, and Scaling Bias
Webinar Series Title: AI and the Legal Profession: Navigating Opportunities and Challenges Webinar Series
Description: This webinar discusses algorithmic decision systems that can curtail access to rental housing and homeownership, accelerate evictions, and increase surveillance and criminalization of people living in public housing.

Sponsor/Group: Section of Civil Rights and Social Justice
Title: AI in Criminal Justice: Automated Decision-making Tools and Technology, From Policing to Corrections
Webinar Series Title: AI and the Legal Profession: Navigating Opportunities and Challenges Webinar Series
Description: This webinar discusses automated decision-making tools used by law enforcement, prosecutors, defense attorneys, judges, and corrections officials and it offers perspectives on the purported benefits and potential harms of the tools.

Title: Data Analytics and the Courts: Essential Information for an Emerging Generative AI Function
Webinar Series Title: Task Force on Law and Artificial Intelligence Webinars
Description: A judge-led roundtable examines how generative AI is reshaping litigation and judicial decision-making. The discussion covers courtroom deployment of analytical tools, best practices, cautionary tales, and insights into preparing the judiciary for an AI-driven future.

Title: AI Governance: A Conversation with Elizabeth Kelly
Webinar Series Title: Task Force on Law and Artificial Intelligence Webinars
Description: Cynthia Cwik and Elizabeth Kelly of the U.S. AI Safety Institute explore trends in AI oversight and governance, addressing global governance structures, risk mitigation strategies, and collaboration among policymakers, technologists, and legal professionals to foster responsible AI.

Sponsor/Group: Section of Civil Rights and Social Justice
Title: A Conversation with Pinterest CEO Bill Ready and CLO Wanji Walcott
Description: Topics will range from the evolution of Pinterests strategy and its vision for the future, how artificial intelligence is impact Pinterest and technology companies, the working relationship between a CEO and CLO, and more.

Title: Persuade or Not to Persuade: Advertising in the Digital Age
Description: How marketing activities trying to persuade consumers to buy products or services differ from the trickery of business practices is unsettled.

Title: Diverse Perspective on AI & Antitrust
Description: Valuable insights on how AI is shaping the antitrust landscape and navigating the complexities of this ever-evolving field.

Title: A Conversation Healthcare ADR Voices of Experience
Description: Katherine Benesch and Arthur Peabody Hooper delve into alternative dispute resolution topics, including contract clauses, negotiation strategies, party expectations, the resolution of attorneys fees, and the integration of artificial intelligence.

Sponsor/Group: Section of Civil Rights and Social Justice
Title: AI in Education: Addressing Biases and Discrimination, Privacy & Surveillance
Webinar Series Title: AI and the Legal Profession: Navigating Opportunities and Challenges Webinar Series
Description: This webinar navigates the complexities of AI in education and explores potential strategies for ensuring that AI enhances, rather than detracts from, educational equity and student well-being.

Sponsor/Group: Section of Civil Rights and Social Justice
Title: AI and Consumers: The Invisible Impact on Economic Justice
Webinar Series Title: AI and the Legal Profession: Navigating Opportunities and Challenges Webinar Series
Description: This webinar include a presentation of the harms created or exacerbated by automated decision systems as they relate to consumer law issues, such as lending and debt collection, and an overview of responses from a recent ABA survey on consumer law.

Title: Unraveling AI’s Impact on Intellectual Property: Expert Perspectives
Webinar Series Title: Task Force on Law and Artificial Intelligence Webinars
Description: An expert panel dissects how generative AI is challenging traditional IP frameworks. They analyze case law implications, ownership questions, authorship rights, and emerging legal standards, offering practitioners practical insights for navigating copyright, patent, and trademark issues in an AI-driven world.

Title: AI Governance and Risk Management - The Role of Lawyers
Webinar Series Title: Task Force on Law and Artificial Intelligence Webinars
Description: Legal leaders Ifeoma Ajunwa, Katherin Fick, Karen Buzard, and Madhu Srikumar share strategies on lawyer-led governance. They outline how attorneys are defining AI policies, overseeing risk frameworks, and guiding clients through responsible AI adoption.

Sponsor/Group: Section of Civil Rights and Social Justice
Title: AI Essentials For Lawyers: What You Need To Know To Protect Your Clients In The Digital Age
Webinar Series Title: AI and the Legal Profession: Navigating Opportunities and Challenges Webinar Series
Description: This webinar explores the basics of AI, contextualizes related definitions as they pertain to the law, and discusses the impact of AI on marginalized communities.

Title: Privacy & AI: Navigating the Legal Landscape
Description: This panel explores legal considerations regarding cloud technologies in the context of privacy and artificial intelligence.

Title: A Roundtable on Generative AI: Practical Advice for Attorneys
Webinar Series Title: Task Force on Law and Artificial Intelligence Webinars
Description: This webinar explores the practical and legal implications of generative AI for law firms and in-house legal teams, including how the technology works, its applications in legal workflows (like contract drafting and document review), and the importance of establishing clear internal policies for its use. Panelists also examine emerging risks and challenges, particularly around intellectual property, data privacy, and ethical use, offering guidance to help legal professionals responsibly integrate generative AI into practice.

Title: Regulating A.I.: Competition Enhancing or a Barrier to Entry?
Description: As A.I. becomes the cornerstone of many software products and startup companies,would regulating A.I. make markets more competitive or would it create barriers to entry and expansion.

Title: Childrens Privacy: What you Need to Know Now and into 2024
Description: At the federal and state levels, efforts to pass childrens privacy laws continue to increase. The Federal Trade Commission has undertaken a review of the rules for the Childrens Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA).

Title: Designing Effective AI Compliance Programs
Description: Program from Media & Technology, Joint Conduct, and Compliance and Ethics Committees on AI compliance programs and ensuring their effectiveness.

Title: AI and Algorithmic Pricing: Is the New Tech Old Hat for Antitrust Enforcement?
Description: Pricing Conduct and Unilateral Conduct committees program on AI-based pricing algorithms and whether they are legal in Antitrust.

Title: Third-Party Risk Management: Legal Best Practices in Al Integration
Description: This session discusses strategies for integrating AI solutions into business operations and vetting Al solutions to ensure compliance with legal and regulatory requirements while mitigating risks in supply chains.

Title: Unlocking the AI Toolbox: Empowering Clients & Practices in Family Law
Description: ChatGPT, Gemini (formerly known as Bard), and other large language models are efficient tools that can be integrated into law practice management to help with marketing, administrative tasks, and better client representation.

Title: The Impact of Deepfakes on the Justice System
Webinar Series Title: Task Force on Law and Artificial Intelligence Webinars
Description: Focused on courtroom implications, this webinar, featuring Hany Farid, Paul Grimm, and Maura Grossman, examines how deepfakes affect trial integrity. It offers tools, detection techniques, legal strategies, and resources to support judges and lawyers confronting deepfake evidence.

Title: Corporate Counseling Monthly Update - December 2023
Description: The December update for in-house counsel on new and ongoing cases. The corporate counseling committee elaborates on new cases in the field and analyzes settlement details.

Title: The Future of International Cartel Investigations
Description: Please join Fried Frank and the Antitrust Cartel Committee for a timely discussion about the future of international cartel investigations.

Title: National Security Committee Meets with Jill Goldenziel
Description: Global evolution and escalation of lawfare, an integration of legal warfare into military and influence operations, including in the South China Sea and the Middle East.

Sponsor/Group: Section of Civil Rights and Social Justice
Title: Artificial Intelligence – The New Frontier
Description: A.I. The New Frontier: Panel of Special Advisors for the ABA Task Force on Law and Artificial Intelligence featuring Ivan Fong, Executive Vice President, General Counsel and Secretary at Medtronic and many more.

Title: Cloud/AI/Privacy: Trends and Legal Implications
Description: This panel will explore legal considerations regarding cloud technologies in the context of privacy, artificial intelligence, and cybersecurity, considering legal, policy and regulatory frameworks in the United States and abroad.

Title: European Approach to AI (Artificial Intelligence) Governance
Description: European Commissions proposed legal framework for regulating the development and deployment of AI (Artificial Intelligence).

Title: Hot Topics III: Not Too Fast, AI! Exploring Legal Ethics and Regulations in the Era of AI-Powered ChatGPT
Description: In an era where artificial intelligence is transforming industries, the legal profession stands on the cusp of a revolutionary shift.

Title: AI & IP: IP Protection for AI-Generated Works: How AI Can Facilitate IP Protection, and Regulation of AI Use
Description: The basics of artificial intelligence, its role in art generation, training the algorithm, the process of generating an image, and the implications for copyrightability, trademark, trade secrecy, and patent.

Title: Its No Game: Legal Issues in Esports
Description: This program will address several legal issues that arise in esports. Panelists will discuss contractual and regulatory challenges both domestically and across borders, gambling, and intellectual property issues.

Title: Practice Area Insights Exploring General Counsel Responsibilities and Trends
Description: GC Matt Boisen discusses major trends affecting both inside and outside counsel, such as the rise of generative AI, and the experience of the GC role.

Title: Corporate Counsel Committee Roundtable Discussion: Corporate Counsels Role in Guiding Client Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence
Description: Well discuss steps to understand whether and how your clients already use generative AI and their potential future uses. Well talk through drafting policies and guidance that help clients understand the risks and best practices for using AI.

Sponsor/Group: Section of Civil Rights and Social Justice
Title: Introducing the A.I. & Economic Justice Survey
Description: This webinar includes introductory remarks by senior ABA members, a walk-through of the A.I. survey, and an open Q&A with the A.I. & Economic Justice team.

Title: Braving the Thucydides Trap: Challenges to Transit Through The Narrowing Straits of CFIUS Reviewed Investments in AI and Other Emerging Technologies
Description: The strategic choices clients and their counsel must plan and execute when pursuing cross-border trade in contested technologies, from a CLE program at the Business Law Section Spring Meeting 2022.

Title: The Final Countdown to the EU AI Act
Description: How the AIAs risk-based governance scheme will create new requirements across a broad scope of entities and jurisdictions, from a CLE program at the Business Law Section Spring Meeting 2022.

Title: Today, Tomorrow, or Never: Ethical, Legal, and Business Considerations for ChatGPT, Bard, Dall-E, and Similar Generative AI
Description: Explores the legal, ethical, and policy considerations regarding the use of generative AI by businesses, their employees, and their vendors, from a CLE program at the Business Law Section Spring Meeting 2022.

Title: Cloud/Privacy/AI: Trends and Legal Implications
Description: This privacy program explores legal considerations regarding cloud technologies in the context of privacy and artificial intelligence, considering legal, policy and regulatory frameworks in the United States and abroad.

Sponsor/Group: Section of Civil Rights and Social Justice
Title: Where Do We Go From Here?: Innovative Solutions and Next Steps
Description: Concluding the Economic Justice Summit, the final panel discusses the lessons weve learned from the two-day conference and what lawyers, advocates, students and anyone interested in making an impact on economic justice can do to help.

Sponsor/Group: Section of Civil Rights and Social Justice
Title: Algorithms, AI, and Disability Discrimination in Hiring: Complying with the ADA
Description: This webinar discusses the EEOCs guidance and the types of algorithmic practices that can show up in the hiring process, how and why these practices impact disabled workers, and best practices for employers.

Title: Practice Area Insights Artificial Intelligence and Its Impact on Business Law: An Introduction
Description: Ingeuneal C. Gray of the American Arbitration Association provides an incisive introduction to AIs power and effect on the legal profession.

Title: Primer on AI Technologies and Definitions
Webinar Series Title: Task Force on Law and Artificial Intelligence Webinars
Description: Tailored towards judges and clerks, this webinar aims to explain core AI concepts and applications in legal work. Topics include generative AI mechanics, contract tools, firm policy development, and IP risk considerations, providing actionable guidance for law firm integration.

Title: AI Governance: A Conversation with Miriam Vogel
Webinar Series Title: Task Force on Law and Artificial Intelligence Webinars
Description: Moderator Cynthia Cwik and Miriam Vogel, President of EqualAI and Chair of the National AI Advisory Committee, discuss global AI oversight, anti-bias efforts, and the intersection of ethics and governance in legal practice.

Title: How Large Law Firms Are Incorporating AI into Practice
Webinar Series Title: Task Force on Law and Artificial Intelligence Webinars
Description: This webinar explores how major law firms adopt AI tools—like automated drafting and predictive platforms—while addressing regulatory and ethical implications. It highlights implementation hurdles, internal policy creation, and AI’s transformative potential in large-scale legal services.

Title: The Implications for Generative AI on Legal Education - A Conversation with Andrew Perlman
Webinar Series Title: Task Force on Law and Artificial Intelligence Webinars
Description: Andrew Perlman and Cynthia Cwik reflect on how generative AI is reshaping legal education. They cover curriculum innovation, skill development for future lawyers, ethical training, and the balance between AI literacy and legal reasoning.

Title: ABA Presidential Speaker Series: Artificial Intelligence - The New Frontier
Webinar Series Title: Task Force on Law and Artificial Intelligence Webinars
Description: A panel featuring Daniel Ho, Michelle Lee, Trooper Sanders, Miriam Vogel, and Seth Waxman discusses the future of AI in law, public policy, ethics, and access, offering a strategic vision for AI’s impact on the legal profession and society.

Title: AI Governance: A Conversation with Reva Schwartz of the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) about NIST's new AI Risk Management Framework
Webinar Series Title: Task Force on Law and Artificial Intelligence Webinars
Description: Cynthia Cwik and National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) research scientist Reva Schwartz explore the NIST AI Framework and how organizations—from firms to regulators—are using it to promote trustworthy and responsible AI systems.

Title: Side Effects May Include: Litigation
Description: This panel explores emerging disputes over provider marketing, pricing transparency, and AI-driven care tools.

Title: AI & Antitrust Series, Episode 5: AI in Expert Work and Litigation
Description: This panel brings together litigators, experts, and a judicial voice to explore where to draw the line between automation and expertise, what disclosure standards are needed, and how to ensure responsible, defensible use of AI in litigation.

Title: Winning the Race: The State of AI Governance and AI Regulations
Description: AI governance is rapidly shifting as federal policy pulls back and states push ahead with divergent regulatory approaches.

Title: AI & Antitrust Series: AI in Healthcare
Description: AI is being deployed in novel ways across the healthcare sector-from diagnosis and care decisions to designing or selecting insurance plans. This panel of speakers discusses the complexeties of these choices.

Title: Groundbreakers Series: Generative AI for Pricing
Description: This session features Professor Maxime Cohen who will discuss Generative AI for Pricing, a topic on which he has researched and written extensively, including in a forthcoming book Pricing in the Age of AI.

Title: Cybersecurity Legal Task Force Monthly Meeting - April 2026
Description: Jane Horvath, a partner in the Washington, D.C. office of Gibson Dunn draws from more than two decades of privacy and legal experience to discuss AI and Cybersecurity issues.

Title: AI & Antitrust Series: Foundation Models and LLMs
Description: This panel will demystify what these technologies are and how they work, explore developing theories of harm and enforcement priorities, and examine how antitrust frameworks can adapt to fast-moving AI innovation.

Title: Emerging AI Tools for Divorce Professionals and Their Clients
Description: This video will explore emerging AI applications relevant to family law practice and demonstrate how these developing tools can support more efficient workflows and expanded service models.

Title: Monopoly Broth: Clear Recipe or Mushy Theory?
Description: This panel of antitrust expert practitioners and academics examine the monopoly broth theory and how it works in practice.

Title: The Hitchhikers Guide to AI (for Beginners)
Description: Discover how to take your first steps into artificial intelligence confidently. This session guides senior lawyers through signing on to AI platforms, choosing the right tools, and exploring practical personal and professional uses.

Title: AI & Antitrust Series: AI Applications
Description: This panel will introduce key genAI applications and explore its disruptive economics, assessing how it may affect competition and innovation across various markets.

Title: The Use of AI to Record, Transcribe, and Summarize Attorney-Client Conversations
Description: A discussion between Roland Trope and Lucian Pera around the NY City Bar Professional Ethics Committee Opinion on the Use of AI to Record, Transcribe, and Summarize Attorney-Client Conversations.

Title: AI & Antitrust Series: Infrastructure and Data
Description: This panel brings together an industry expert, economist, FTC attorney, and defense counsel to discuss the basics of GPUs, compute, and data servers, and to explore emerging global antitrust issues in AI infrastructure, including access.

Title: AI in State and Local Procurement
Description: This session will explore emerging challenges and opportunities in the procurement of artificial intelligence technologies, as well as the use of AI tools to streamline procurement processes and enhance data security at the state and local levels.

Title: AI and Antitrust: A Technical Primer
Description: AI Practitioner will explain key AI concepts for antitrust lawyers-how systems are built, the roles of data and models, and design tradeoffs.

Title: Targeted Price Discrimination
Description: This program will cover three key questions: Is targeted price discrimination merely the digital-age realization of classic first-degree price discrimination, or does it represent a fundamentally new economic paradigm?

Title: AI in Discovery: Real-World Litigation Applications
Description: Featuring a diverse panel of experts, the discussion explores how AI is being used to streamline workflows, surface patterns in massive datasets, and enhance strategic decision-making.

Sponsor/Group: Section of Civil Rights and Social Justice
Title: AI & Legal Ethics: What You Should Know About Impacts on the Profession
Description: An exploration of how AI is reshaping legal ethics, access to justice, and practice norms, offering grounded insight into emerging risks, professional duties, and opportunities for innovation.

Title: A New Frontier of AI Readiness: How Legal Employers are Adopting AI and What it Means for New Lawyers
Description: Hear how big law, small law, and courts are implementing AI technology, the policies and procedures they deploy to safeguard ethical and effective AI use, and what expectations they have for AI among new lawyers.

Title: Role-Based Containment for Agentic AI: Embedding Agency in Caregiving Systems
Description: RBC ensures safe, ethical, and accountable AI in dementia care, protecting dignity and trust within the evolving dynamic data economy.

Title: Autonomous Vehicles & the Emerging Duty of Care: From Neural Simulations to Courtroom Strategy
Description: As autonomous vehicles hit public roads, courts are redefining duty of care. Liability evidence may now include not just traditional records but also neural networks, live software updates, and large sets of simulated training data.
